raw_input:
[4.0/5] cannot be alone done I have an aunt in another state who is a hair dresser. Years ago, she asked me if I wanted to perm my lashes when she came down to visit. I thought she was crazy! This was like 10 years ago. I never heard of such a thing and I didn't do it. Well I don't know what happened recently for this to suddenly now become popular. One of my friends and I were talking about lash extensions and lattisse. I was telling her how my eye lids turn red when i try to use lattisse. I even tried the RF brand and same thing! Anyhows, then i've been doing exentions for about a year now but I am finding that my lash is getting worst and worst.. like when my extensions fall out, I barely have lashes! I used to have average lash that I could curl. But now it seems like I barely have anything. So this is why I decided to finally consider perming my lash. So perming the did not make it longer by any means! What it did though is made it look like I had lashes that were longer but it curved up! My lash tends to grow straight. It may have have to do with the fact that I have asian eyes. So the curling has always been important when I used to wear mascara. The thing is I hate that sometimes it poked at me and the curler would yank some lashes out! Also I just hate it doing it every day. With perm lashes, you don't have to do this. However, I don't know if I could have done this alone. I had to get a friend to help me. She said it was pretty easy. What took the longest is us reading things over and over to make sure we weren't going to make me blind! I think it took about an hour or so? I can't recall exactly as we didn't time it. The reason I am giving it 4 stars is that you really cannot do this by yourself. Now it may not look drastic to most people bc they don't usually notice your lashes. But you would b/c you know how they look before. Then add mascara and you can definitely tell. [4.0/5] Nice Kit Overall, this is a pretty decent kit if you want to forgo a trip to the salon. It has all of the products you need to complete your lash perm, however there is a bit of a learning curve to it. Once you get the hang of it however, it goes pretty smoothly. I myself have used several different kits, and this one ranks in my top 5. The results were pretty good and there was noticeable lifting of my lashes. I estimate I will be able to get roughly 3 perms out of this kit in total which is pretty good, especially considering the price. It says it can last upwards of 4 weeks, but I guess only time will tell how long the perm actually lasts. Regardless, you cant beat the price for an at home DIY. [4.0/5] First time will take 3 hours. Buqikma Lash Lift Kit Eyelash Perm Kit Lash Curling Glue Set Semi-Permanent Curling Perming Wave for Salon and Personal Usehey

There's many kits like this online, and I'm pretty sure they're all the same. Some come with extra things but they're pretty low quality, and you're better off with a kit like this, and preparing your own tools.

It works. It's really hard to do on yourself. It saves you money from going to a salon. It takes forever. It doesn't really hurt. (But I'm sure it's really bad if you get it in your eye, how could it not be?).

If you have a friend who will do it on you, it would be better just to get two eyes done at the same time. But there's still a learning curve. Doing it on myself, I hated my first 2 perms, but my 3rd came out okay.

Also it doesn't say if you have to have your eyes closed, I tried to do one eye closed and it was impossible with the eye twitches of having one eye open. Everyone on youtube had their eyes open, and it didn't say on the package, so when I tried with my eyes open, it came out 100x better.

I recommend you gather Q-tips, napkins, and toothpicks. If you have long lower eyelashes, you can use medical tape or you can kill two birds with one stone and use a under eye mask. But I have long lower lashes and did fine without either one. Get the glue all over the pad, make sure you stick your tips on there too. Use the comb of the cleaner tool to fan out the lashes nicely once they're stuck. I also heard you can use a lash curler before hand to help it stick up, but don't crimp too hard. [5.0/5] Working great Working great, no allergies, easy to use, last long. In my picture I have already two weeks that I made one side to check if theres any changes. So far so good [4.0/5] Not Perfect, But It Works This product is not without its flaws, but if you are patient enough it will work. My biggest issue was with the glue. I had the hardest time trying to get my lashes to actually stick to the pad. It took a long time, and even then I had some that just refused to stay. All in all it probably took me an hour to do one eye (I’d strongly recommend doing each eye individually if you’re doing them yourself). The other recommendation I’d give is to watch some YouTube tutorials before starting. The instructions are bad, but they aren’t great and there are a lot of videos out there to help ensure you’re doing things correctly. Overall, my lashes were lifted and I’m fairly happy with the result. It wasn’t as dramatic as I hoped (you can see that with mascara my lifted lashes look the same as my curled lashes) but I do think it looks nice. If you’re looking for a cheap diy alternative to the salon I’d say try it out. But if you have the time and money, I’d opt for a professional service instead. [5.0/5] Works but takes a little practice They come labeled in steps. It’s easier to watch the video instead of the instructions. I recommend doing one eye at a time if doing it on yourself. Smaller lift pads allow you to get a tighter curl. Larger pad means looser curl. This kit works! Wish they had stronger eyelash glue—always takes me a lot of swipes to keep lash on the pad so I use my own eyelash glue. Otherwise, wonderful product!! [4.0/5] Same results as salon perm This is my 3rd time purchasing this perming kit. I would have given it 5 stars except for the fact that you only receive enough product to do 2 permings, and if you don't carefully and quickly seal the bottles of solutions back up with the little rubber stoppers along with the lids the product will go bad and will not be good for a 2nd use. That has been my experience each time with this product. I continue to purchase it because it does work effectively the first time and even if I only get one use out of it, at this price I am still getting my lashes permed cheaper than going to a salon. The perm lasts on me for about 8 weeks. [4.0/5] Lifts lashes well!! Worked well!!! A little awkward to apply. Need to let the glue dry a little prior to applying. [1.0/5] Waste of money Did not curl my eye lashes. Product is not good. I used everything else with another product. [1.0/5] Didn’t work Lifted my lashes for one day then they fell. Glue is not strong at all. Would not hold my lashes to the rubber lid part.. I think that’s probably why the lift didn’t work very well [5.0/5] The key is patience Honestly this stuff is a PAIN to get the hang of but once you do, the results speak for themselves!!!
It took me about 4 tries before I got it, and on the 4th try i learned that i had to do 1 eye at a time to get it to work properly so I can use the other eye to see what the heck i'm doing so it took twice as long as the instructions say. Nonetheless, I loved the results and this is my second box. Results lasted me around 4 weeks in this gorgeous state then weeks 5-6 it dimmed down a little more each day. I used this as an alternative to individual lash glue ons since those started to take a toll on my lashes and I dont think ill go back to glue ons.
Get them! [1.0/5] DO NOT BUY DO NOT BUY THIS!!! I had really high hopes for this product. When I received it, the pads to put on your eyelids do not fit. they are extremely too big, even the small ones. Then, when I tried to apply the glue to my lashes it just weighed them down and did not stick at all, so when I went to put the perm on all of it got into my lashes and eyes and started to burn super bad. not even sure what products are in this so i hope nothing happens to my eyes. my advice would just to get your eyelashes professionally done. Because all the time and hassle I wasted trying to use this product I could have had someone else do it for me. [1.0/5] Porquería No funciona y me creo reacción en mis ojos de ardor y comezón [4.0/5] Leave perm on for longer and it’ll do the trick I liked that this lash lift gave MULTIPLE options for how curled you wanted your lashes. I will say the lashes need more time in the perm section than what is mentioned. If I stick to the original time, my lashes barely curl. Overall, I like it! [1.0/5] Eyelash glue sucks dont buy The eyelash glue sucks. If it actually made the pad or eyelash stop th iui s product might actually work [4.0/5] Try it! Used to laminate brows-Works! Instructions are not great but there are plenty of tutorials to help. Didn't irritate or cause redness [5.0/5] Easy to use I love it it works perfectly ,easy to use !

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, customers have mixed opinions about this lash lift kit. Many reviewers found that it does work and produces noticeable results, with lifted and curled lashes that can last anywhere from 4 to 8 weeks. Several customers noted it is a cost-effective alternative to salon visits, and some have purchased it multiple times. A few reviewers also found success using it for brow lamination.

However, there are some consistent complaints and challenges. The biggest recurring issue is with the glue, which many customers found weak and difficult to work with, making it hard to keep lashes stuck to the pads. The process has a steep learning curve and is very difficult to do alone, with most reviewers recommending having a friend help or doing one eye at a time. The instructions were considered poor by several reviewers, and most suggested watching YouTube tutorials instead. The process can take anywhere from one to three hours, especially for beginners.

A few customers had negative experiences, including burning and irritation in their eyes, pads that were too large to fit properly, and results that did not last. Some felt the product simply did not work at all.

The general consensus is that the kit can work well if you are patient and willing to practice, but those who want guaranteed results or have limited time may be better off visiting a professional salon.
